constraints: The game plays itself unless the player intervenes at the right moment
Only a single button controls everything
The player controls the enemies, not the hero
No UI or HUD is allowed at any point
You grow weaker as you approach the goal
Time moves in reverse — death rewinds, not resets
You can only move when enemies move
Exactly 3 colors only: black, white, and one accent
No explicit win state — the player must recognize when they've won
The camera is the primary obstacle, not the enemies
Every resource you collect permanently reduces your speed
Every action has a mandatory 3-second delay before executing
wildcards: You can only undo, never redo
All SFX must be made with your voice — no samples
The level procedurally destroys itself as you play
NPCs give at least one piece of intentionally bad advice
The score is always hidden from the player
Victory requires self-sacrifice
Controls are never displayed — the player must discover them
The protagonist cannot directly interact with enemies

constraints: No jumping allowed under any circumstances
Gravity flips direction every 30 seconds
The entire game plays in reverse
Player and level move simultaneously, never independently
Enemies become temporary allies when defeated
Only 10 total lives for the entire run — no refills
All controls are permanently mirrored left to right
You can only see what the character can logically see from their position
Resources are shared between player and enemies
No direct attacks — only environmental hazards deal damage
Score is your health — points heal, damage removes score
Death rewinds time exactly 10 seconds
wildcards: All dialogue plays as reversed audio
The color palette shifts permanently at the game's midpoint
The narrator is unreliable and knows it
The boss encounter is disguised as a tutorial
Every object in the world has an audible heartbeat
Player actions leave permanent visible marks on the level
All music is diegetic only — no background score
Death restores a portion of health to the player

constraints: Each ability can only be used exactly once per run
Any mistake permanently resets the score multiplier to zero
You cannot look directly at enemies — only their shadows or reflections
The map reveals only areas the player has already visited
Failure adds time to the clock instead of removing it
No traditional respawn — death leaves behind a ghost ally
Your weapon is also your only movement mechanic
Ambient light slowly drains health over time
Collecting items permanently reduces maximum movement speed
The level layout shifts completely every time the player dies
The score multiplier is the only visible health indicator
Saving progress costs resources the player must earn
wildcards: The level editor is part of the gameplay loop
The antagonist provides live voiceover commentary
Physics values drift by 25% every 60 seconds
Saving costs resources the player may not have
The tutorial sequence is mandatory on every single run
All menus appear as physical objects in the game world
The game renders at an intentionally low resolution
Death spawns a ghost that becomes a temporary ally

constraints: All controls randomize completely every 10 seconds
The game runs at exactly one frame per second
The player leaves a damaging trail wherever they move
The screen rotates 90° every 60 seconds
Score counts down from a maximum — hold on to what you have
Enemies receive the player's inputs with a 3-second delay
The camera updates 3 seconds after the player actually moves
Every action has a 50% chance of doing the exact opposite
The HUD always displays incorrect values
Checkpoints deal damage instead of restoring health
Gravity direction is voted on by NPCs every 30 seconds
Every third player input is silently discarded
wildcards: All developer commentary is intentionally wrong
The UI actively works against the player
Unlocking any achievement permanently disables one mechanic
Difficulty settings are labeled in reverse order
The win screen appears on loss, and vice versa
The map rearranges itself every 45 seconds
A random game event fires every 15 seconds
All SFX are from a completely different game genre
